question: BitBoy convicted?
description:
BitBoy was recently arrested due to a warrant issued for threatening communications he sent to a Judge. You can read more about that here: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/22059661159362

This market will resolve to "Yes" if BitBoy (Ben Armstrong) is convicted of any charges related to the emails he sent to Judge Kimberly Childs, by March 31, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Convictions must be for criminal charges.

If the case is completely dropped for whatever reason, due to a plea agreement without an admission of guilt, a dismissal, or any other situation occurs in which no judgement is rendered or will be rendered by the court, this market will resolve to "No".

Once an individual is convicted it qualifies for an immediate "Yes" resolution, regardless of later appeals, etc. This market will resolve based on the first conviction in a qualifying case.

The primary resolution source will be official court records, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Russia-Ukraine Ceasefire before GTA VI?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if  if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution for a ceasefire will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Take-Two Interactive's August 8 earnings call solidified Grand Theft Auto VI's Fall 2025 release window—fiscal 2026 for the publisher—quelling delay fears and stabilizing trader consensus on pre-launch timelines. Rockstar's first trailer, a December 2023 cultural juggernaut with over 200 million YouTube views, sustains massive hype, but no second trailer or substantive updates have surfaced in the past 30 days. Industry dynamics favor Rockstar's tight-lipped strategy, mirroring past hits like Red Dead Redemption 2, though gaming delays loom as an upset risk. Watch Tokyo Game Show (September) and Take-Two's November earnings for catalysts that could accelerate trailer buzz or precursor events like Nintendo Switch 2 reveals.

question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump ceases to be the President of the U.S. for any period of time before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

An announcement of Trump's resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, will not count.

The resolution source for Trump leaving the presidency will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will bitcoin hit $1m before GTA VI?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTCUSDT) has a final "High" price of $1,000,000 or higher before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

The resolution source for Bitcoin is Binance, specifically the BTCUSDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTCUSDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if it Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-unanimous confidence (97.9% implied probability) that GTA VI will miss a pre-June 2026 launch, driven primarily by Take-Two Interactive's official slippage of the release window from an initial 2025 target to May 26, 2026, as confirmed in their most recent earnings call. Rockstar Games' history of extended polishing phases for blockbusters like Red Dead Redemption 2, which faced multiple delays, bolsters this view, alongside the lack of a second trailer 22 months after the debut and reports of ongoing development hurdles. With real capital at stake, traders see little room for acceleration in this late-cycle crunch. Realistic upsets remain slim but could include an unprecedented early trailer drop with an advanced date or internal leaks proving ahead-of-schedule progress ahead of the next fiscal update.

question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects deep uncertainty in Harvey Weinstein's legal saga, with No Prison Time edging out at 27.5% amid his New York rape conviction's April 2024 overturn and a retrial slated for spring 2025, alongside new sex crime indictments. The closely bunched frontrunners—10-20 years (23.3%) and 20-30 years (20.1%)—hinge on his upheld 16-year California sentence and ongoing appeals, tempered by recent health crises including bone cancer surgery in September 2024 that spurred compassionate release speculation. Key differentiators include retrial witness credibility, appeal rulings from higher courts, and medical evaluations, as #MeToo-era public sentiment clashes with procedural wins eroding prior momentum. Traders eye bail hearings and trial delays as potential swings in this high-stakes Hollywood reckoning.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Harvey Weinstein prison time?

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects deep uncertainty in Harvey Weinstein's legal saga, with No Prison Time edging out at 27.5% amid his New York rape conviction's April 2024 overturn and a retrial slated for spring 2025, alongside new sex crime indictments. The closely bunched frontrunners—10-20 years (23.3%) and 20-30 years (20.1%)—hinge on his upheld 16-year California sentence and ongoing appeals, tempered by recent health crises including bone cancer surgery in September 2024 that spurred compassionate release speculation. Key differentiators include retrial witness credibility, appeal rulings from higher courts, and medical evaluations, as #MeToo-era public sentiment clashes with procedural wins eroding prior momentum. Traders eye bail hearings and trial delays as potential swings in this high-stakes Hollywood reckoning.

question: Will OpenAI launch a new consumer hardware product by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI publicly announces and launches a new consumer hardware product by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

A "consumer hardware product" is defined as a physical device intended for direct use by individual consumers, rather than enterprise or developer tools. The product must be newly introduced and not a rebrand, update, or iteration of any previously released device.

Examples that would qualify include an AI-powered wearable, smart home assistant, or augmented reality device. Developer tools, AI chips, or servers marketed exclusively for enterprise use would not qualify.

The resolution source will be official information from OpenAI.

events.title: Will OpenAI launch a consumer hardware product by...?
events.description: This is a market on whether OpenAI will release a consumer hardware product by the specified date.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has fueled trader optimism by confirming in recent interviews that the company is actively developing consumer hardware, including a potential screenless AI device combining phone, computer, and wearable functionalities, amid reports of a high-profile partnership with Jony Ive's design firm and acquisition of hardware startup io. These developments, building on mid-2024 hiring sprees of former Apple engineers, position OpenAI to challenge flops like the Humane AI Pin and Rabbit R1 by integrating advanced large language model capabilities directly into physical products. However, with no official launch timeline or prototypes demonstrated, market-implied odds reflect uncertainty; watch for catalysts like OpenAI DevDay announcements or funding updates that could solidify competitive hardware positioning before year-end resolution.

question: Will Italy qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This is a market on which teams will qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to qualify for the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g. they cannot reach the required number of points to advance from its group or qualify for playoffs), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or the qualifying stage has not been completed by September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be FIFA.  (https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup).

events.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Which countries qualify?
events.description: This is a market on which teams will qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

eventMetadata.context_description: Only six nations have secured spots in the expanded 48-team 2026 FIFA World Cup: co-hosts Canada, Mexico, and the United States, plus AFC standouts Iran, Japan, and Uzbekistan, who topped their third-round groups last month with Uzbekistan clinching via a 2-0 win over Qatar on November 14. CONMEBOL frontrunners Argentina (already qualified since June), Brazil, Uruguay, Paraguay, Ecuador, and Colombia strengthened positions through recent matchday results, including Paraguay's surge past Venezuela. UEFA's campaign launches March 2025 for 16 direct slots plus playoffs, while CAF group stage upsets—like South Africa's climb—and CONCACAF battles beyond hosts intensify. Key factors include goal difference edges, head-to-head ties, and final-round fixtures through 2025, feeding into intercontinental playoffs.

question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices the Colorado Avalanche highest at 19.7% implied probability to win the 2026 Stanley Cup, driven by their intact elite core of Hart Trophy winner Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, and recent five-year extension for depth center Casey Mittelstadt amid an improving early-season form. Tampa Bay Lightning trail closely at 14.1%, fueled by Nikita Kucherov's MVP-contender production and Andrei Vasilevskiy's elite goaltending in a 11-7-1 start, while Carolina Hurricanes (10.5%) and Dallas Stars (8.4%) benefit from deep rosters, strong defensive systems, and playoff pedigree. Buffalo Sabres' 6.4% reflects surging optimism around young stars Tage Thompson and Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's goaltending. The bunched top reflects NHL parity, with cap crunches, prospect development, trade deadlines, and injury volatility keeping 25+ teams in contention for the long 2025-26 campaign.

Tampa Bay Lightning trail closely at 14.1%, fueled by Nikita Kucherov's MVP-contender production and Andrei Vasilevskiy's elite goaltending in a 11-7-1 start, while Carolina Hurricanes (10.5%) and Dallas Stars (8.4%) benefit from deep rosters, strong defensive systems, and playoff pedigree. Buffalo Sabres' 6.4% reflects surging optimism around young stars Tage Thompson and Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's goaltending. The bunched top reflects NHL parity, with cap crunches, prospect development, trade deadlines, and injury volatility keeping 25+ teams in contention for the long 2025-26 campaign.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus for the 2026 NBA Champion at 36.5% implied probability, driven by their explosive 10-2 start to the 2024-25 season atop the Western Conference standings, fueled by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber scoring (29+ PPG) and Chet Holmgren's two-way dominance alongside Isaiah Hartenstein's rim protection addition. San Antonio Spurs sit at 15.3% on Victor Wembanyama's sophomore surge (averaging 24 PPG, 11 RPG early) and Chris Paul's veteran playmaking stabilizing the young core post-2024 draft. Boston Celtics hold 12.4% as reigning champions with Jayson Tatum and Jrue Holiday intact, though a tougher path looms amid rising Eastern competition from Cleveland Cavaliers (5.0%) and others; Denver Nuggets (8.5%) rely on Nikola Jokic amid backcourt questions. Odds reflect offseason roster upgrades and early momentum shaping long-term futures.

question: MegaETH market cap (FDV) >$2B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of MegaETH's token is greater than $2,000,000,000 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 24 hours after launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If MegaETH doesn't launch a token by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

events.title: MegaETH market cap (FDV) one day after launch?
events.description: This is a market on MegaETH FDV

question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's commanding Euro 2024 triumph, capped by a final win over England and featuring breakout stars like Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams, has traders pricing them as the narrow favorite amid their UEFA Nations League dominance in recent matches. England remains close behind, buoyed by a deep Premier League-fueled squad and consistent major tournament runs despite the final loss. France and Argentina follow tightly, with Kylian Mbappé's post-injury form and the defending champions' Copa América success sustaining momentum. Brazil's talent pool keeps them competitive despite Copa struggles. This bunched trader consensus reflects the 2026 tournament's expanded 48-team format, grueling confederation qualifiers across CONMEBOL, AFC, CAF, and CONCACAF, and 18 months until the North American-hosted kickoff, leaving ample room for form shifts, injuries, and surprises.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's commanding Euro 2024 triumph, capped by a final win over England and featuring breakout stars like Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams, has traders pricing them as the narrow favorite amid their UEFA Nations League dominance in recent matches. England remains close behind, buoyed by a deep Premier League-fueled squad and consistent major tournament runs despite the final loss. France and Argentina follow tightly, with Kylian Mbappé's post-injury form and the defending champions' Copa América success sustaining momentum. Brazil's talent pool keeps them competitive despite Copa struggles. This bunched trader consensus reflects the 2026 tournament's expanded 48-team format, grueling confederation qualifiers across CONMEBOL, AFC, CAF, and CONCACAF, and 18 months until the North American-hosted kickoff, leaving ample room for form shifts, injuries, and surprises.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's commanding Euro 2024 triumph, capped by a final win over England and featuring breakout stars like Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams, has traders pricing them as the narrow favorite amid their UEFA Nations League dominance in recent matches. question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's unprecedented consolidation of authority as China's paramount leader, cemented by the 2018 abolition of presidential term limits and his unchallenged third term as Communist Party General Secretary in 2022, drives the 91.3% implied probability that he remains in power through 2027. Recent Central Committee plenary sessions in July 2024 emphasized "Xi Jinping Thought" guiding economic reforms and technological self-reliance, while ongoing anti-corruption purges of military and party officials signal continued dominance absent credible rivals. State media portrays his active role in diplomacy, including recent meetings with global leaders, with no verified health concerns or succession signals. Trader consensus reflects structural barriers to removal, though rare scenarios like a sudden medical event or internal coup could alter odds ahead of the 2027 Party Congress.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Kamala Harris's defeat in the 2024 presidential election, the Democratic 2028 nomination field remains wide open, with California Governor Gavin Newsom commanding a 24% trader consensus lead on Polymarket due to his national media presence, aggressive fundraising exceeding $100 million early, and positioning as a pragmatic progressive counter to Republican gains. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8% on her progressive star power and social media influence among young voters, while Jon Ossoff's 6% reflects his youth and Georgia swing-state Senate victory. Key differentiators include executive experience for Newsom, ideological appeal for AOC, and battleground credentials for Ossoff; consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm performances, party unity post-mortems, and early primary state polling.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Kamala Harris's defeat in the 2024 presidential election, the Democratic 2028 nomination field remains wide open, with California Governor Gavin Newsom commanding a 24% trader consensus lead on Polymarket due to his national media presence, aggressive fundraising exceeding $100 million early, and positioning as a pragmatic progressive counter to Republican gains. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8% on her progressive star power and social media influence among young voters, while Jon Ossoff's 6% reflects his youth and Georgia swing-state Senate victory. Key differentiators include executive experience for Newsom, ideological appeal for AOC, and battleground credentials for Ossoff; consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm performances, party unity post-mortems, and early primary state polling.

question: Putin out as President of Russia by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Vladimir Putin ceases to be President of Russia for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Vladimir Putin's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Vladimir Putin and the government of Russia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Putin out as President of Russia by December 31, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Vladimir Putin ceases to be President of Russia for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Vladimir Putin's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Vladimir Putin and the government of Russia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vladimir Putin's entrenched grip on power, reinforced by his March 2024 re-election amid suppressed opposition and constitutional amendments enabling service until 2036, anchors trader consensus at 87.5% against his removal by December 31, 2026. No recent political catalysts—such as elite defections, health crises, or no-confidence challenges—have emerged to shift sentiment, with Russia's security apparatus and siloviki remaining loyal. Ongoing Ukraine conflict developments, including Moscow's counteroffensive reclaiming much of the Kursk region invaded in August and North Korean troop deployments in October, signal Putin's sustained wartime leadership without domestic instability. Barring unforeseen coups, scandals, or health events with slim historical precedent, traders anticipate continuity through his current term ending 2030.

question: Will JD Vance win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

events.title: Presidential Election Winner 2028
events.description:
The 2028 US Presidential Election is scheduled to take place on November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the person who wins the 2028 US Presidential Election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same candidate. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same candidate by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: JD Vance holds a slim edge over Gavin Newsom in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election winner, reflecting post-2024 positioning where Vance's vice presidential role under a potential Trump administration elevates his national profile and path to the GOP nomination, while Newsom's high-visibility governorship in California positions him as a leading Democratic contender amid the party's leadership vacuum. The tight race among top candidates like Marco Rubio underscores the early-stage uncertainty, with no primaries underway and room for new entrants, scandals, or midterm outcomes in 2026 to shift dynamics. Trader sentiment balances strong incumbency-like advantages for Vance against Newsom's fundraising prowess and progressive appeal in battleground states, keeping probabilities clustered below 20%.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player is officially named the 2025–26 NBA Rookie of the Year. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If the award is not announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the NBA (https://www.nba.com).

events.title: NBA Rookie of the Year
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Duke freshman Kon Knueppel at 71.9% implied probability for NBA Rookie of the Year, propelled by his explosive early-season form including a 28-point outburst on efficient shooting against strong competition, outshining projected top pick Cooper Flagg, who sits at 28.5% amid a solid but turnover-prone start and minor ankle tweak in practice. Knueppel's sharpshooting (50%+ from three) and seamless fit in Duke's rotation highlight his NBA translatability as a wing scorer, while Flagg's defensive prowess awaits offensive consistency. The field trails at 0.1% each, reflecting their uneven debuts or delayed impacts in college rotations, with no major injuries or transfer portal shifts altering the hierarchy yet.

question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by March 31, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty of no Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by March 31, 2026, driven by the protracted military stalemate and irreconcilable demands after nearly three years of invasion, escalation, and failed diplomacy. Recent Russian advances in Donetsk amid intensified airstrikes and Ukraine's stalled Kursk incursion highlight ongoing battlefield momentum favoring Moscow, with Putin rejecting talks absent territorial concessions and recognition of annexed regions like Crimea and Donbas. Zelenskyy maintains preconditions of full troop withdrawal to 1991 borders, bolstered by continued Western arms and sanctions. No active peace negotiations have materialized in the past 30 days, reinforcing skepticism. Potential shifters include a major diplomatic breakthrough under the incoming Trump administration, economic pressures prompting concessions, or unexpected leadership changes, though historical precedents like Minsk agreements suggest formidable barriers.

question: Will Donald Trump win the 2028 Republican presidential nomination?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump's cabinet nominations during the post-election transition have propelled Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to the top of trader consensus for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination at 49%, reflecting his high-profile HHS secretary pick on November 14 and alignment with MAGA priorities on health policy and skepticism of federal agencies. Vice President-elect J.D. Vance follows at 36.6%, bolstered by his role as the party's presumed heir apparent after Trump's 2024 victory, with incumbency advantages in key swing states like Ohio. Marco Rubio's 21.1% share stems from his recent Secretary of State nomination, enhancing his foreign policy credentials and appeal to establishment donors. These early administration roles signal insider momentum, though primaries remain distant amid shifting alliances, endorsements, and potential scandals; historical precedents show vice presidents win nominations only about 30% of the time.

Vice President-elect J.D. Vance follows at 36.6%, bolstered by his role as the party's presumed heir apparent after Trump's 2024 victory, with incumbency advantages in key swing states like Ohio. Marco Rubio's 21.1% share stems from his recent Secretary of State nomination, enhancing his foreign policy credentials and appeal to establishment donors. These early administration roles signal insider momentum, though primaries remain distant amid shifting alliances, endorsements, and potential scandals; historical precedents show vice presidents win nominations only about 30% of the time.

question: Will Ken Paxton win the 2026 Texas Republican Primary?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Texas. 

If no 2026 Texas Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Texas Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Texas Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Texas. 

If no 2026 Texas Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Texas Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Attorney General Ken Paxton's 56% implied probability as Texas Republican Senate primary winner reflects trader consensus on his strong appeal among the GOP base, fueled by his aggressive lawsuits against federal policies and acquittal in the 2023 impeachment trial that solidified Trump-aligned support. Incumbent Senator John Cornyn trails at 43% amid criticism from conservatives over his bipartisan votes on Ukraine aid, debt ceiling deals, and brief gun measure consideration post-Uvalde. Recent polls, such as the University of Houston Hobby School's September survey, show Paxton edging Cornyn among likely primary voters, with high undecideds amplifying entry risks. Minor candidates like Dawn Buckingham, Beth Van Duyne, and Wesley Hunt lack traction. Official announcements loom ahead of the March 2026 primary.

eventMetadata.context_description: Attorney General Ken Paxton's 56% implied probability as Texas Republican Senate primary winner reflects trader consensus on his strong appeal among the GOP base, fueled by his aggressive lawsuits against federal policies and acquittal in the 2023 impeachment trial that solidified Trump-aligned support. Incumbent Senator John Cornyn trails at 43% amid criticism from conservatives over his bipartisan votes on Ukraine aid, debt ceiling deals, and brief gun measure consideration post-Uvalde. Recent polls, such as the University of Houston Hobby School's September survey, show Paxton edging Cornyn among likely primary voters, with high undecideds amplifying entry risks. Minor candidates like Dawn Buckingham, Beth Van Duyne, and Wesley Hunt lack traction. Official announcements loom ahead of the March 2026 primary.

question: Will the Democratic Party control the Senate after the 2026 Midterm elections?
description:
This market will resolve according to the party that controls the Senate following the 2026 U.S. Senate elections scheduled for November 3, 2026.

Senate control is defined as having more than half of the voting Senate members, or half of the voting Senate members and the Vice Presidency.

If the outcome of this election is ambiguous given the above rules, this market will remain open until the Majority Leader of the US Senate is selected following the 2026 US general election, at which point it will resolve to the party the majority leader is affiliated with at the time of their election to that position. If the elected majority leader does not caucus with any listed party this market will resolve “Other”.

Determination of which party controls the Senate after the 2026 US Senate elections will be based on a consensus of credible reporting, or if there is ambiguity, final federal and/or state election authority certification or other final official determination of the 2026 election results.

events.title: Which party will win the Senate in 2026?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Republican traders hold a narrow edge at 51.5% for retaining Senate control in 2026 midterms, driven by a lopsided map where the party defends 22 mostly safe seats compared to Democrats' 13, including competitive battlegrounds like Georgia (Jon Ossoff), Michigan (Gary Peters), Minnesota (Tina Smith), New Mexico (Martin Heinrich), and Virginia (Mark Warner). This terrain requires Democrats to net four flips for a majority—challenging given recent Republican gains to a 53-47 edge post-2024. The tight consensus reflects midterm history, where the president's party (GOP under Trump) averages 3.5 seat losses, alongside uncertainties in turnout, fundraising, and early race ratings from Cook Political Report labeling most GOP holds as solid or likely. Primaries starting mid-2025 and economic trends could widen the gap.

question: Will the Democratic Party control the House after the 2026 Midterm elections?
description:
This market will resolve according to the party that controls the House of Representatives following the 2026 U.S. House elections scheduled for November 3, 2026.

House control is defined as having more than half of the voting members of the U.S. House of Representatives.

If the outcome of this election is ambiguous given the above rules, this market will remain open until the Speaker of the House is selected following the 2026 U.S. general election, at which point it will resolve to the party the Speaker is affiliated with at the time of their election to that position. If the elected Speaker does not caucus with any listed party this market will resolve “Other”.

Determination of which party controls the House after the 2026 U.S. House elections will be based on a consensus of credible reporting, or if there is ambiguity, final federal and/or state election authority certification or other final official determination of the 2026 election results.

events.title: Which party will win the House in 2026?

Determination of which party controls the House after the 2026 U.S. House elections will be based on a consensus of credible reporting, or if there is ambiguity, final federal and/or state election authority certification or other final official determination of the 2026 election results.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders strongly favor Democrats (84%) to win House control in the 2026 midterms, driven by the historical midterm penalty against the president's party, which has lost an average of 28 seats since World War II and seats in every cycle since 2006. Republicans hold a fragile 220-215 majority after narrow 2024 victories in battleground districts, leaving them vulnerable to swings in swing states like Pennsylvania and Michigan. Early generic ballot polls show Democrats leading by 3-5 points amid economic concerns and Trump administration policy debates, boosting opposition turnout prospects. Key upcoming factors include potential GOP retirements, special elections, and redistricting challenges in competitive seats, underscoring the closely contested path to a Democratic majority.

question: 2026 Balance of Power: D Senate, D House
description:
This market will resolve according to the result of the 2026 United States midterm elections.

A party will be considered to have 'control' of the House of Representatives, if they win a majority of voting seats.

A party will be considered to have 'control' of the Senate if they have more than half of the voting Senate members, or half of the voting Senate members and the Vice President.

A candidate's party is determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time the 2026 United States midterm elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican Parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party that they most recently expressed their intent to caucus with at the time the 2026 United States midterm elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

If control of the House is ambiguous given the above rules, this market will resolve according to the party affiliation of the first Speaker of the US House who is selected following the 2026 United States midterm elections. 

If control of the Senate is ambiguous given the above rules, this market will resolve according to the party affiliation of the first Majority Leader of the US Senate who is selected following the 2026 United States midterm elections.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources have conclusively called winners of the House and Senate in the 2026 United States midterm elections. If all three sources do not achieve consensus in calling the relevant races for this market, it will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Democrats regaining the House amid historical midterm penalties for the president's party, with implied probabilities showing an 84% chance of Democratic House control versus 15% for Republicans retaining it. Early 2025 generic ballot polls, including Emerson College's D+8 and YouGov's D+5 leads, underscore this sentiment, amplified by the GOP's slim five-seat House majority vulnerable to the average 26-seat loss for incumbents. Senate control remains a toss-up near 50/50, reflecting Republicans defending 22 seats on a favorable map including safe red states, though competitive races in Maine and North Carolina add uncertainty. Recent GOP House retirements and Democratic fundraising edges have boosted the Democrats Sweep outcome to 48.5%, while R Senate/D House at 35.5% captures split control expectations ahead of 2026 primaries.

Senate control remains a toss-up near 50/50, reflecting Republicans defending 22 seats on a favorable map including safe red states, though competitive races in Maine and North Carolina add uncertainty. Recent GOP House retirements and Democratic fundraising edges have boosted the Democrats Sweep outcome to 48.5%, while R Senate/D House at 35.5% captures split control expectations ahead of 2026 primaries.

question: SCOTUS accepts sports event contract case by July 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Supreme Court of the United States grants certiorari in a case explicitly concerning the legality, regulation, or jurisdictional authority over sports event contracts by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A case qualifies if it addresses at least one of the following: (1) whether contracts based on sporting event outcomes constitute regulated derivatives under the Commodity Exchange Act; (2) whether federal regulation via the Commodity Futures Trading Commission preempts state-level gambling laws as applied to such contracts; or (3) whether sports event contracts offered by federally licensed markets may legally be offered, restricted, or prohibited by federal or state authorities.

The certiorari grant must be publicly confirmed via the official SCOTUS docket or orders list, and verifiable through credible legal reporting or the Supreme Court’s official website. The case does not need to be heard, scheduled, or decided to qualify. 

The resolution source will be a consensus census of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: The U.S. Supreme Court recently received a petition for writ of certiorari in a case challenging the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) classification of proposed "event contracts" on sports outcomes as prohibited gaming under the Commodity Exchange Act. Filed on October 15, 2024, by a coalition including prediction market operators, the petition seeks review of the D.C. Circuit's July 2024 decision upholding the CFTC's denial. No major updates have emerged since filing, but traders should monitor the CFTC's response deadline around November 29, followed by potential distribution to justices and conference votes in early 2025. Granting certiorari remains uncertain, hinging on the Court's interest in clarifying CFTC authority amid ongoing prediction market litigation like Kalshi's election contracts win.

question: Will the Cleveland Cavaliers win the NBA Eastern Conference Finals?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Cleveland Cavaliers win the 2025–2026 NBA Eastern Conference Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2025–26 NBA Eastern Conference Finals based on the rules of the NBA.

If the 2025-26 NBA Eastern Conference Finals winner is not announced by June 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: NBA Eastern Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Eastern Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Boston Celtics at 35.6% implied probability to win the NBA Eastern Conference, driven by their defending champion pedigree, elite depth led by Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown, and strong road form despite recent minor injury hiccups for Kristaps Porzingis. Cleveland Cavaliers sit at 20.5% following their league-best 23-4 start fueled by Donovan Mitchell's scoring surge and Evan Mobley's two-way dominance, though a slight dip in recent games tempers enthusiasm. New York Knicks (16.5%) gained traction post-Karl-Anthony Towns trade boosting frontcourt scoring alongside Jalen Brunson, while Detroit Pistons (13.5%) surged on Cade Cunningham's MVP-caliber play during a nine-win-in-11-games streak. Lower odds reflect Bucks' and 76ers' key injuries to Giannis Antetokounmpo, Damian Lillard, Joel Embiid, and Paul George, alongside inconsistent form for Heat, Magic, and others amid playoff races tightening.

events.title: NBA Western Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Western Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder command 49.5% trader consensus as Western Conference favorites after clinching the No. 1 seed with the NBA's top record, fueled by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP frontrunner stats (30+ PPG, elite defense) and Chet Holmgren's rim protection amid a nine-game win streak entering the playoffs. San Antonio Spurs' 23.2% reflects their blistering six-wins-in-eight-games surge, powered by Victor Wembanyama's historic rookie impact (27 PPG, 3+ blocks recently) and upsets over Denver and the Lakers, vaulting them into play-in contention. Denver Nuggets linger at 10.5% despite Nikola Jokic's dominance, hampered by Jamal Murray's hamstring injury and a 3-4 record over their last seven. Lakers' 6.3% ties to LeBron James and Anthony Davis availability post-back-to-back wins, while Minnesota Timberwolves fade to 4.5% after defensive lapses and play-in risks.

question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

events.title: Which party wins 2028 US Presidential Election?
events.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Democrats a 57.5% implied probability over Republicans at 42.5% to win the 2028 US presidential election, reflecting President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory and resulting term limits under the 22nd Amendment barring his third run, which opens a wide Republican primary field. Recent early polling aggregates show Democratic governors like Gavin Newsom and Gretchen Whitmer topping hypothetical matchups against VP JD Vance and other GOP figures, amid post-election analysis of narrow popular vote margins and voter turnout patterns favoring Democrats in non-swing states. The 2026 midterms represent the next major catalyst, historically boosting the out-party's presidential path, with no significant shifts in trader sentiment over the past week.

question: Will PSG win the 2025–26 Champions League?
description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: UEFA Champions League Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 26.5% implied probability for Champions League glory, fueled by their flawless league phase finish and clinical round-of-16 aggregate win over PSV Eindhoven, showcasing defensive solidity and Saka's resurgence. Bayern Munich trails closely at 22.5% after dominating Shakhtar and Benfica in knockouts, with Kane and Musiala driving Bundesliga form. Barcelona's 16.5% reflects Hansi Flick's tactical revival and Lewandowski's scoring streak past Benfica, while PSG's 12.5% stems from Enrique's Ligue 1 dominance despite Dembele injury concerns. Defending champs Real Madrid sit at 9.5% amid Vinicius Jr.'s dip and a tough Arsenal quarterfinal draw, keeping the field wide-open with volatile knockout matchups ahead in the last eight.

events.title: English Premier League Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's commanding position atop the Premier League table, with a multi-point lead and superior goal difference after 14 matches, drives trader consensus at 88.5% implied probability for the title, reflecting their unbeaten run in recent fixtures including key wins over rivals and a robust defensive record under Mikel Arteta. Manchester City trails at 11.5% amid inconsistent form, injuries to key midfielders like Rodri, and a congested schedule, while Liverpool, Manchester United, and Aston Villa languish far back due to dropped points and squad disruptions. Challenges to Arsenal could emerge from major injuries to stars like Bukayo Saka or Martin Ødegaard, a grueling run-in against top-four sides, or City mounting an improbable late surge leveraging their historical comeback prowess in title races.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to win the league (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's commanding position atop the Premier League table, with a multi-point lead and superior goal difference after 14 matches, drives trader consensus at 88.5% implied probability for the title, reflecting their unbeaten run in recent fixtures including key wins over rivals and a robust defensive record under Mikel Arteta. Manchester City trails at 11.5% amid inconsistent form, injuries to key midfielders like Rodri, and a congested schedule, while Liverpool, Manchester United, and Aston Villa languish far back due to dropped points and squad disruptions. Challenges to Arsenal could emerge from major injuries to stars like Bukayo Saka or Martin Ødegaard, a grueling run-in against top-four sides, or City mounting an improbable late surge leveraging their historical comeback prowess in title races.

events.title: La Liga Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 La Liga (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's commanding lead atop the La Liga table with 38 points from 15 matches—six clear of Real Madrid—anchors trader consensus at 76.5% implied probability for the title, fueled by an eight-match unbeaten streak capped by a 2-1 win over Real Betis last weekend and Robert Lewandowski's 15 goals. Real Madrid's 22.5% reflects their resilience despite dropping points in a 4-4 thriller at Villarreal and Vinícius Júnior's ongoing muscle injury recovery, which tested their attack in midweek Champions League action. Villarreal (0.3%) and Atlético Madrid (0.1%) trail far in the standings with inconsistent form and mounting fixture pileups, leaving the title race a two-horse contest as the winter transfer window nears.

events.title: Serie A League Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 Serie A league (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Inter Milan commands an 83.5% implied probability as Serie A frontrunners, holding a seven-point lead atop the table after 15 matches with 37 points, bolstered by a Serie A-best +22 goal difference and just one league loss. Recent dominance includes a 2-0 win over Parma on December 6 and a gritty 2-1 victory against Udinese last weekend, showcasing Lautaro Martínez's scoring form (12 goals) and Simone Inzaghi's tactical edge amid title defense. Napoli (4.8%) trails closely at 30 points but faltered with a 1-0 loss to Atalanta, denting momentum despite Antonio Conte's rebuild. AC Milan (10.9%) sits fifth on 27 points, hampered by inconsistent form and injuries to key players like Theo Hernández, while promoted Como's surprise run fades against elite squads. Traders see Inter's depth, home strength, and favorable run-in as near-insurmountable in the Scudetto race.

events.title: Bundesliga Winner
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 Bundesliga (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich commands a 13-point lead atop the Bundesliga table with just two matchdays remaining, driving trader consensus to a 98.9% implied probability for the title amid their unmatched goal difference and relentless form. Recent dominance includes a 4-0 thrashing of Heidenheim last weekend, coupled with dropped points from distant chasers—RB Leipzig lost 2-3 to Frankfurt, Borussia Dortmund drew 2-2 at home to Mainz, VfB Stuttgart fell 1-3 to Leipzig, and Hoffenheim managed only a 1-1 stalemate against Wolfsburg—effectively extinguishing the title race. Barring an unprecedented Bayern collapse with injuries to stars like Harry Kane or Jamal Musiala, plus perfect rival results, no realistic path exists for challengers to overhaul them.

question: Will Trump pardon Ghislaine Maxwell by end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ghislaine Maxwell receives a presidential pardon, commutation, or reprieve from Donald Trump between July 23, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If it becomes impossible for Trump issue a federal pardon, commutation, or reprieve within this market's timeframe, it may immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for whether a person is pardoned or not will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Trump pardon Ghislaine Maxwell by end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ghislaine Maxwell receives a presidential pardon, commutation, or reprieve from Donald Trump between July 23, 2025 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If it becomes impossible for Trump issue a federal pardon, commutation, or reprieve within this market's timeframe, it may immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for whether a person is pardoned or not will be official information from the US government, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President-elect Donald Trump's lack of any public statements or transition team signals regarding a pardon for Ghislaine Maxwell drives the 93.5% implied probability against it occurring by end-2026. Maxwell, convicted in 2022 of sex trafficking minors in connection with Jeffrey Epstein and sentenced to 20 years, saw her conviction upheld by a federal appeals court on September 17, 2024, diminishing prospects for reversal. Trump's previous pardons centered on political allies and January 6 defendants, with no precedent for high-profile sex crime cases that could invite backlash. Absent a formal request or late-breaking developments like health issues or new legal twists, traders see formidable barriers amid inauguration priorities.

question: Will the next Prime Minister of Hungary be Viktor Orbán?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next Prime Minister of Hungary
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are expected to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the individual who is next officially appointed and confirmed as Prime Minister of Hungary following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally elected and appointed to the role of Prime Minister. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is confirmed by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Hungary; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Péter Magyar leads trader consensus at 62.5% implied probability to become Hungary's next prime minister, driven by his Tisza Party's sustained national polling edge over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz following Tisza's strong 30% showing in June's European Parliament elections—its first major outing—and Orbán government scandals, including a leaked recording of judicial interference that propelled Magyar's anti-corruption platform. Orbán holds at 37.5% as the entrenched incumbent with institutional advantages ahead of the scheduled April 2026 parliamentary election under the current mixed majoritarian-proportional system, where Fidesz retains rural strongholds and media dominance. Minor candidates like László Toroczkai trail far behind, reflecting fragmented opposition dynamics, though no snap election or no-confidence vote is currently signaled. Traders weigh upcoming debates and economic pressures as potential catalysts.

question: Will China invade Taiwan by end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of the Republic of China (Taiwan) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Territory under the administration of the Republic of China including any inhabited islands will qualify, however uninhabited islands will not qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be will be official confirmation by China, Taiwan, the United Nations, or any permanent member of the UN Security Council, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will China invade Taiwan by end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of the Republic of China (Taiwan) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Territory under the administration of the Republic of China including any inhabited islands will qualify, however uninhabited islands will not qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be will be official confirmation by China, Taiwan, the United Nations, or any permanent member of the UN Security Council, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 89.3% implied probability against a Chinese invasion of Taiwan by end of 2026, driven by the absence of mobilization signals, formidable military deterrents, and Beijing's economic constraints. Recent PLA drills around Taiwan in late October 2024, following President Lai Ching-te's National Day speech, simulated blockades but remained routine posturing without invasion precursors like amphibious assault preparations or troop surges. Diplomatic channels persist via US-China defense talks and Xi Jinping's APEC remarks emphasizing "peaceful reunification" over force. US arms sales, including advanced missiles, and alliances like AUKUS strengthen Taiwan's defenses, while China's slowing growth and property crisis diminish risk appetite for high-stakes conflict. No major escalations in the past 30 days sustain this low-probability positioning.

question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders price a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by end-2026 at just 33.5% ("Yes"), reflecting a military stalemate reinforced by recent Russian advances recapturing over half of Ukraine's Kursk salient launched in August, alongside high casualties on both sides amid Ukraine's ammunition shortages and Russia's bolstered forces with North Korean troops. No diplomatic breakthroughs have emerged, with President Zelensky insisting on full Russian withdrawal, NATO membership, and reparations, while Putin demands recognition of annexed territories and Ukraine's neutrality. Absent active peace talks or major de-escalation signals, the wisdom of crowds anticipates prolonged attrition, though the U.S. presidential election on November 5 could alter aid flows and negotiation dynamics.

question: Netanyahu out by end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Benjamin Netanyahu ceases to be Prime Minister of Israel for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Benjamin Netanyahu and the government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Netanyahu out by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Benjamin Netanyahu announces that he will resign as Prime Minister of Israel, or otherwise steps down from/is removed from this position by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation or removal before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the State of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu remains in office amid mounting political pressure following the International Criminal Court's issuance of arrest warrants against him on November 21, 2024, for alleged war crimes in Gaza operations. Intensifying protests marking the first anniversary of the October 7 Hamas attack demand hostage deals, early elections, and his resignation, fueled by polls showing his Likud party trailing far behind Benny Gantz's National Unity in hypothetical Knesset votes. His slim coalition holds a 64-seat majority in the 120-seat parliament, but far-right partners like Itamar Ben-Gvir threaten collapse over stalled Gaza offensives and budget disputes—failure to pass the 2025 budget by March could trigger snap elections. Ongoing wars with Hamas and Hezbollah, plus U.S. President-elect Trump's incoming support, sustain trader uncertainty on no-confidence votes or judicial challenges.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine's ongoing martial law, extended multiple times since Russia's full-scale invasion in February 2022, constitutionally bars presidential elections, anchoring trader consensus at 79.5% against Zelenskyy exiting office by end-2026. Zelenskyy's approval ratings remain solid in recent KIIS and Razumkov polls (around 60-70% in late 2024), bolstering his wartime leadership amid steady Western aid flows, including a fresh €35 billion EU package in October. No viable challengers have emerged, and official statements reaffirm elections only post-martial law lift, likely years away given stalled frontline advances in Donbas. Incoming U.S. leadership under Trump adds uncertainty via potential peace negotiations, but historical precedents favor term continuity during active conflict. Traders price low odds of snap developments like a no-confidence push or health issues forcing early exit.

question: Erdoğan out by December 31, 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's second term, secured in the May 2023 election, constitutionally runs through 2028, with no snap election or no-confidence mechanisms available under current rules, anchoring trader consensus at 86.5% against his exit by December 31, 2026. Recent local elections in March 2024 saw opposition CHP gains in major cities like Istanbul, yet Erdoğan's AKP retains parliamentary majority and institutional control, including over judiciary and media. Ongoing military operations in Syria and diplomatic engagements, such as summits with regional leaders, signal robust leadership without health or scandal disruptions. Opposition remains fragmented post-Ekrem İmamoğlu's legal challenges, with no viable path to early ouster amid economic pressures. Upcoming 2028 presidential race looms distant.

question: Will Trump be impeached by end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes“ if the US House of Representatives by simple majority vote to approve or pass one or more articles of impeachment of President Donald Trump, between July 24, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Neither trial nor conviction by the US Senate, nor removal from office, is necessary to resolve this market to “Yes“.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the federal government of the United States, however a consensus of credible reporting will be used.

events.title: Will Trump be impeached by end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes“ if the US House of Representatives by simple majority vote to approve or pass one or more articles of impeachment of President Donald Trump, between July 24, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Neither trial nor conviction by the US Senate, nor removal from office, is necessary to resolve this market to “Yes“.
