eventMetadata.context_description: Republican control of both chambers of Congress following the 2024 elections has solidified trader consensus against impeachment of President Trump by the end of 2026, with "No" shares reflecting an 88% implied probability. The GOP's House majority blocks initiation of articles of impeachment absent defections or a midterm flip, while the Senate's 53-seat Republican edge makes the required two-thirds conviction vote (67 seats) mathematically improbable even if Democrats regain the House in November 2026. Recent cabinet confirmations and focus on Trump's policy agenda, including executive actions on immigration and trade, have shifted attention from past controversies like January 6 investigations, with no fresh catalysts for impeachment proceedings emerging in the past month. Midterm outcomes remain a key uncertainty for traders.

question: Will Trump resign by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of the United States Donald Trump announces he has resigned or will resign the presidency by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If it becomes impossible for Donald Trump to resign or to announce his resignation (e.g., due to his removal from office by other means, etc.), this market will immediately resolve to "No."

For this market to resolve to "Yes," it is only necessary that Trump announce that he has resigned or will resign. Whether he actually resigns will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Trump resign by December 31, 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: President-elect Donald Trump's vigorous transition efforts, including rapid cabinet nominations and executive action planning, underpin the 93.5% trader consensus on "No" for resignation by December 31, 2026. Recent dismissal of federal cases against him post-election, absence of health disclosures, and unified Republican control of Congress eliminate typical pressures like impeachment or party revolt seen in rare historical resignations such as Nixon's. No official statements or leaks suggest intent to step down early in his second term, reflecting low political risk through mid-2026 barring unforeseen scandals, legal appeals, or personal events.

question: Will Scottie Scheffler win the 2026 Masters tournament?
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler tops trader consensus at 16.5% implied probability as world No. 1 and defending champion, bolstered by elite strokes gained approach numbers (key for Augusta's demanding greens) and a T3 finish at last week's Valero Texas Open, signaling sharp form despite a prior hand injury scare. Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau share 7.5% as stylistic fits—Rory's career grand slam motivation and tee-to-green dominance post-Dubai win, DeChambeau's prodigious driving and T6 here last year amid strong LIV results. Jon Rahm (6.6%) trails on solid major pedigree but uneven Augusta finishes; Ludvig Aberg (5.3%) impresses with runner-up debut last year. Wide-open field reflects variable course history, mild weather forecast with Thursday rain potential, and no dominant recent winner among top contenders.

Jon Rahm (6.6%) trails on solid major pedigree but uneven Augusta finishes; Ludvig Aberg (5.3%) impresses with runner-up debut last year. Wide-open field reflects variable course history, mild weather forecast with Thursday rain potential, and no dominant recent winner among top contenders.

question: Will Jordan Spieth win the 2026 Masters tournament?
description:
This polymarket will resolve according to the player who wins the 2026 Masters Tournament.

If this player is eliminated from contention for The Masters based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by The Masters official tournament rules.

If no winner is announced by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution sources will be the official results published by the PGA TOUR website and The Masters website. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Scottie Scheffler tops trader consensus at 16.5% implied probability as world No. 1 and defending champion, bolstered by elite strokes gained approach numbers (key for Augusta's demanding greens) and a T3 finish at last week's Valero Texas Open, signaling sharp form despite a prior hand injury scare. Rory McIlroy and Bryson DeChambeau share 7.5% as stylistic fits—Rory's career grand slam motivation and tee-to-green dominance post-Dubai win, DeChambeau's prodigious driving and T6 here last year amid strong LIV results. Jon Rahm (6.6%) trails on solid major pedigree but uneven Augusta finishes; Ludvig Aberg (5.3%) impresses with runner-up debut last year. Wide-open field reflects variable course history, mild weather forecast with Thursday rain potential, and no dominant recent winner among top contenders.

question: Will Vicky Dávila win the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the greatest number of valid votes in the first round of voting.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Iván Cepeda Castro as a dominant 70% favorite to win Colombia's 2026 presidential election first round, driven by his prominence as a Pacto Histórico senator and perceived continuity candidate for President Gustavo Petro's agenda amid recent left-wing unity signals in senate debates on peace process extensions and social reforms. Paloma Valencia holds 22%, buoyed by Centro Democrático's opposition surge following government struggles with security escalations, including Clan del Golfo clashes in early October 2024, and economic policy setbacks. With primaries unheld and the March 2026 first round distant, odds reflect early speculation; upcoming candidate announcements, national polling averages, and regional endorsements could shift the competitive field of challengers like Vicky Dávila and Sergio Fajardo.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Candidate M as narrow frontrunner at 49.5¢ implied probability to win Colombia's 2026 presidential election, with Paloma Valencia (42.3¢) and Iván Cepeda Castro (40.5¢) keeping the race tight amid a fragmented 19-candidate field. President Gustavo Petro's sinking approval ratings below 30%—driven by stalled reforms, rising violence in border regions, and economic stagnation—have boosted opposition challengers, but no single figure has consolidated support in recent Invamer and Datexco polls showing top contenders clustered in the low-to-mid teens. Right-wing vote-splitting between Valencia's Uribista base and Candidate M's appeal sustains the deadlock, while Cepeda represents leftist regrouping post-Petro. Party primaries and consultas in early 2025, plus potential scandals or endorsements, could tip the first-round dynamics before the May 2026 vote.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls for Colombia's 2026 presidential election, such as the November Guarumo survey and October Invamer data, show the top candidates—Vicky Dávila at 22%, Claudia López at 20%, and others like Miguel Uribe below 15%—far short of the 50%+1 threshold needed for a first-round outright win, amid a splintered field of over a dozen contenders across ideological lines. This fragmentation echoes historical patterns since the 1991 constitution, with every election proceeding to a June runoff due to divided voter support. President Petro's 30% approval has failed to unify backing for any successor, while upcoming party consultations could further dilute leads, solidifying trader consensus on an 88.5% implied probability of "No" first-round victor.

question: Aaron Taylor-Johnson announced as next James Bond?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the above actor is officially announced as the next James Bond actor by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on the first official announcement of who will be the next James Bond, regardless of any changes made thereafter.

If no actor is announced as the next Bond within the timeframe, this market will resolve to "No Bond chosen".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Amazon MGM Studios. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Next James Bond actor?
events.description: This is a market on who will be the next actor to play James Bond in the upcoming film series.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no James Bond actor being chosen soon at 63% implied probability, driven by Eon Productions head Barbara Broccoli's recent interviews confirming no casting discussions amid Bond 26's distant 2026–2027 production timeline under Amazon MGM Studios. This prolonged uncertainty has sidelined frontrunner hopes despite persistent rumors. Callum Turner leads actor odds at 21%, buoyed by his rising profile in prestige projects like Masters of the Air and betting market momentum on sites like Betfair, though unverified tabloid speculation dominates without official screen tests or announcements. Other contenders like Jacob Elordi and Aaron Taylor-Johnson trail on similar unconfirmed buzz, with historical Eon secrecy underscoring high unpredictability until director selection, potentially led by Denis Villeneuve rumors. Watch for studio updates post-holidays.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no James Bond actor being chosen soon at 63% implied probability, driven by Eon Productions head Barbara Broccoli's recent interviews confirming no casting discussions amid Bond 26's distant 2026–2027 production timeline under Amazon MGM Studios. This prolonged uncertainty has sidelined frontrunner hopes despite persistent rumors. Callum Turner leads actor odds at 21%, buoyed by his rising profile in prestige projects like Masters of the Air and betting market momentum on sites like Betfair, though unverified tabloid speculation dominates without official screen tests or announcements. Other contenders like Jacob Elordi and Aaron Taylor-Johnson trail on similar unconfirmed buzz, with historical Eon secrecy underscoring high unpredictability until director selection, potentially led by Denis Villeneuve rumors. Watch for studio updates post-holidays.

question: Tom Hardy announced as next James Bond?
description:

question: Will Liverpool finish in 2nd place in the 2025-26 English Premier League?
description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – 2nd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's 81.5% implied probability to finish 2nd in the Premier League reflects trader consensus on Liverpool's commanding title lead, solidified by the Reds' 1-0 victory over City on December 1 via Cody Gakpo's late strike, extending their advantage to five points atop the table with a +21 goal difference from 14 matches. Arsenal sit second on 29 points from 13 games but face headwinds from Bukayo Saka's multi-week hamstring absence and recent dropped points, including a 1-1 draw at Manchester United, eroding their edge despite a game in hand. City's Rodri has returned from ACL injury, bolstering midfield control amid 20 matches left, while Manchester United's 3.9% nods to their four-match unbeaten run under Ruben Amorim, though they trail far in standings. Lower probabilities for Chelsea, Aston Villa, and others underscore the top trio's dominance in the runners-up race.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – 2nd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's 81.5% implied probability to finish 2nd in the Premier League reflects trader consensus on Liverpool's commanding title lead, solidified by the Reds' 1-0 victory over City on December 1 via Cody Gakpo's late strike, extending their advantage to five points atop the table with a +21 goal difference from 14 matches. Arsenal sit second on 29 points from 13 games but face headwinds from Bukayo Saka's multi-week hamstring absence and recent dropped points, including a 1-1 draw at Manchester United, eroding their edge despite a game in hand. City's Rodri has returned from ACL injury, bolstering midfield control amid 20 matches left, while Manchester United's 3.9% nods to their four-match unbeaten run under Ruben Amorim, though they trail far in standings. Lower probabilities for Chelsea, Aston Villa, and others underscore the top trio's dominance in the runners-up race.

events.title: English Premier League – 3rd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 3rd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester United commands 57.5% trader consensus for finishing third in the Premier League table, fueled by their emphatic 3-0 victory over Leicester City on November 10, signaling momentum under interim management ahead of Ruben Amorim's December arrival, with traders betting on his tactical overhaul mirroring past Sporting triumphs and a favorable run-in featuring winnable home fixtures. Liverpool sits at 20.5% despite leading the standings at 27 points from 11 games, as their eight-point cushion positions them for top-two contention over third. Chelsea (11.9%) and Aston Villa (11.1%) remain competitive threats, buoyed by solid early form and squad depth, though recent draws and injuries to key midfielders temper expectations amid congested schedules and tougher opponents ahead.

Liverpool sits at 20.5% despite leading the standings at 27 points from 11 games, as their eight-point cushion positions them for top-two contention over third. Chelsea (11.9%) and Aston Villa (11.1%) remain competitive threats, buoyed by solid early form and squad depth, though recent draws and injuries to key midfielders temper expectations amid congested schedules and tougher opponents ahead.

question: Will Tottenham finish in last place in the 2025-26 English Premier League?
description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will finish in last place in the 2025–26 English Premier League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes in last place in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish in  last place in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically unable to), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – Last Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in last place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Wolves lead trader consensus at 53% implied probability to finish last in the Premier League, rooted in their winless start with just 1 point from 7 matches, a league-worst -13 goal difference after conceding 17 goals, highlighted by a 2-1 home defeat to Brighton on October 20 amid persistent defensive frailties and scoring droughts under Gary O'Neil. Burnley follows closely at 44.6%, sentiment driven by their dismal 2023-24 relegation campaign with only 3 wins and ongoing questions over squad quality despite topping the Championship table early this term with strong recent form. Leeds and others lag, buoyed by solid Championship positioning and promotion hopes, underscoring the market's focus on bottom-table stagnation and historical struggles.
